plainEnglish: Every page needs a unique, descriptive <title> that says what the page is about. The title is the first thing a screen reader announces and what shows in browser tabs, history, and bookmarks, so 'Untitled' or a generic site name on every page leaves users lost.
whyItMatters: Screen reader users rely on the page title—announced first on load—to know which page they're on; a missing or generic title leaves them disoriented, especially with many tabs open.
quickCheck: Check the browser tab text and the <title> element and confirm it's non-empty and describes this specific page (axe's document-title rule).
commonMistakes: A missing or empty <title> element (axe document-title).
Using the same generic title (like just the site name) on every page.
In single-page apps, never updating document.title on route changes.
Putting the brand name first so the unique part is cut off in the browser tab.

codeExamples.label: Generic title repeated on every page
